title: Daya Nand Charit
gloss: A pamphlet directed against Swami Daya Nand, the founder of the Arya Samaj.

author: Dit Singh, Bhai
title: Daya Nandi T'alim
gloss: Or a book directed against the teachings of Daya Nand Saraswati, the founder of the Arya Samaj, translated into Urdu by Har Chand Singh.
notes: The late Bhai Ditt Singh's Singh Sabha polemic against Dayananda, posthumously translated Punjabi→Urdu — and printed at the Arya Steam Press, the opponent's own shop; the press economy is more ecumenical than the content.

author: Farzand Ali, Munshi
title: Farzand-i-Ali
gloss: A controversial book written in refutation of another entitled 'Ibrahim' on Christ's ascention to heaven.
publisher: The author, who is Head Clerk, Qila Magazine, Ferozepore City
notes: Ascension-of-Christ dispute (the core Ahmadiyya controversy) fought by an ordnance-depot head clerk — office employees as theological combatants.

author: Ganga Ram, Doctor
title: Ruhani Daulat
gloss: Or Spiritual Wealth, which contains detached papers on the social, moral and spiritual development of man.

title: Hayat-ul-Ambiya
gloss: A translation by Mirza Muhammad Nazir of Allamah Jalal-ul-din Sayuti's Intibah-ul-Azkiya in which the writer tries to prove that prophets are alive in their graves, quoting the Quran and traditions.
notes: Al-Suyuti on hayat al-anbiya — Barelvi-leaning doctrinal position in the intra-Sunni polemic; translator Mirza Muhammad Nazir also did s80 and s91 (a one-man classical-translation pipeline for bookseller Fateh Muhammad).

title: Injil-i-Barnabas
gloss: The Gospel of St. Barnabas, translated into Urdu by Maulvi Muhammad Halim from the Arabic version by Dr. Khalib Beck Saadat of Egypt.
copyright: M. Muhammad Insha-Ullah, Lahore; No. 17—, dated the 2—th October, 1910.
notes: The Gospel of Barnabas in Urdu, via Khalil Bey Saadat's 1908 Cairo Arabic edition — polemical scripture moving Cairo→Lahore within two years; second Insha-Ullah/Watan project this quarter (with Jadid Turki Daur, p28 s5): pan-Islamic print internationalism in one editor's list.

flags.issue: register No. and day partially cut at page edge

title: Izalat-ul-Unud
gloss: A translation by Mirza Muhammad Nazir of Qazi Sanaullah Pánipati's pamphlet, in which the writer tries to prove that music is allowed by the Muhammadan religion and that absolute monism as professed by the Sufis is in accordance with the tenets of Islam, quoting the Quran and traditions.
notes: Qazi Sanaullah Panipati (d. 1810) defending the permissibility of music (sama') and wahdat al-wujud, translated into Urdu in the same quarter as the harmonium manual (p26 s183) and Rag Binod (p18 s2) — the theological, pedagogical, and commercial fronts of the 1910 music question all visible at once; core evidence for the music-colonization thread.

title: Maraka-i-Mazhab-o-Science
gloss: Or an Urdu translation by M. Zafar Ali Khan of Draper's 'Conflict between Religion and Science.'
copyright: M. Zafar Ali Khan, B.A., Karmabad; No. 183, dated the 21st November, 1910.
notes: Zafar Ali Khan (later of Zamindar fame) translating J. W. Draper's History of the Conflict between Religion and Science, 504 pp., copyrighted — Victorian scientism absorbed into Urdu religious debate; filed by the registrar under RELIGION, not Science.
